chunks.text: Stereo. HC JD A 38. Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, BAHAWALPUR BENCH, BAHAWALPUR. JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 (The State Vs. Muhammad Sultan ) Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 (Muhammad Sultan Vs. The State.) Date of hearing: 29.09.2025. Appellant by: Mr. Zafar Iqbal Sheikh, Advocate (learned defence counsel appointed at State expense). State by: Mr. Muhammad Riaz, Deputy Prosecutor General. Complainant by: Mr. Muhammad Ishaq, Advocate. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J. – Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda (convict) was tried by the learned Judge, Anti-Terrorism Court, Bahawalpur Division, Bahawalpur, in case F.I.R. No. 354 of 2022 dated 30.09.2022 registered at Police Station Saddar Rahim Yar Khan District Rahim Yar Khan in respect of offences under sections 302, 324,336- B, 109, 435 and 427 P.P.C. and under section 7 of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Gul Naz wife of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased), Shehnaz daughter of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) and Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 27.06.2023 convicted Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda (convict) and sentenced him as under:- Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda: i) Death under section 302(b) P.P.C.as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Gul Naz wife of Khursheed Ahmad Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 2 (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.500,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment.

No. 354 of 2022 dated 30.09.2022 registered at Police Station Saddar Rahim Yar Khan District Rahim Yar Khan in respect of offences under sections 302, 324,336- B, 109, 435 and 427 P.P.C. and under section 7 of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Gul Naz wife of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased), Shehnaz daughter of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) and Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 27.06.2023 convicted Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda (convict) and sentenced him as under:- Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda: i) Death under section 302(b) P.P.C.as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Gul Naz wife of Khursheed Ahmad Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 2 (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.500,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. ii) Death under section 302(b) P.P.C.as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Shehnaz daughter of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.500,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. iii) Death under section 302(b) P.P.C.as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.500,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. iv) Death under section 7 (a) of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Gul Naz wife of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) and directed to pay fine of Rs.200,000/- and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. v) Death under section 7 (a) of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Shehnaz daughter of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) and directed to pay fine of Rs.200,000/- and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. vi) Death under section 7 (a) of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased) and directed to pay fine of Rs.200,000/- and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment.

The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. v) Death under section 7 (a) of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Shehnaz daughter of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) and directed to pay fine of Rs.200,000/- and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. vi) Death under section 7 (a) of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased) and directed to pay fine of Rs.200,000/- and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further one year of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. vii) Rigorous imprisonment of seven years under section 435 P.P.C. and directed to pay fine of Rs.100,000/- and in Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 3 case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. viii) Rigorous imprisonment of two years under section 427 P.P.C. and directed to pay fine of Rs.100,000/- and in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further two months of simple imprisonment. All the sentences awarded to the convict were ordered to run concurrently by the learned trial court. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda (convict) lodged Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 assailing his conviction and sentences. The learned trial court submitted Reference under section 374 Cr.P.C. read with section 30 of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997, which has been numbered as Capital Sentence Reference No. 02 of 2023 for confirmation or otherwise of the death sentences awarded to Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda (appellant). We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 and the Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the facts necessary, as stated by Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), the complainant of the case, are as under: - “Stated that I am cultivator and also runs grocery shop in a house. I had got married my daughter Shehnaz with Sultan Ahmad, accused present in court, 12/13 years prior to this occurrence. Shehnaz Bibi had one daughter aged 09 year and son Aman Ullah aged 02 year from said wedlock, who are alive.

I had got married my daughter Shehnaz with Sultan Ahmad, accused present in court, 12/13 years prior to this occurrence. Shehnaz Bibi had one daughter aged 09 year and son Aman Ullah aged 02 year from said wedlock, who are alive. About 09 months prior to occurrence Sultan Ahmad accused expelled my daughter with children from his house and she started living with me at my house. I made efforts to reconciliate and after failing, my daughter filed suit of dissolution and maintenance allowance for her and her children in the court. Sultan Ahmad accused, present in court, nourished grudge and extended threats to me many times that he would kill us. On 30.9.2022, I brought medicine of my wife Gul Naaz. My sister-in-law Basra Bibi had also come at my home when at about 12:30 day time I was present at Pul Gharib shah I saw accused Sultan Ahmad going towards my house rashly having bottle of petrol in his hand. I also came towards my house behind him, My wife Gul Naaz. my daughter Shehnaz and my sister-in-law Basra Bibl were sitting in my shop and in my view accused Mohammad Sultan, present in court, threw petrol upon my wife Gul Naaz, my daughter Shehnaz and my sister-in-law Basra Bibi and set them on fire. Due to which Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 4 said ladies became burnt and fire also burnt the articles available in my shop. On my hue and cry Amjed Iqbal, Shah Raza and other neighbours also attracted there. Sultan Ahmad extended threats if anybody came near to him, he would also burn him. Accused Mohammad Sultan ran away from the spot. I and neighbours extinguished the fire. My wife Gul Naaz Bibi succumbed to the Injuries at the spot, whereas Shehnaz and Basra were seriously injured due to burn. We made call on Rescue 15. This occurrence was committed by accused Sultan Ahmad on the instigation of Mohammad Iqbal, Amir Sohail and Bilal Asghar residents of Ashraf Abad.

We made call on Rescue 15. This occurrence was committed by accused Sultan Ahmad on the instigation of Mohammad Iqbal, Amir Sohail and Bilal Asghar residents of Ashraf Abad. Ghulam Farid and Mohammad Farhan informed me that today at about 10:00 am they were sitting at hotel situated at Thalli chowk where Amir Sohail, Bilal Asghar and Mohammad Iqbal were instigating their brother Mohammad Sultan to set my daughter and wife on fire and they were asked him that they would handled the matter. Mohammad Sultan committed instant occurrence on their instigation which created sense of fear and insecurity among the public. On arrival of police. I presented application Exh.PC which bears my signature Exh.PC/1 whereon instant F.I.R was registered and police investigated the case.” 4. After the formal investigation of the case report under section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 was submitted before the learned trial court wherein the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda was sent to face trial. The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 19.01.2023 to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. The prosecution in order to prove its case got statements of as many as twenty three witnesses recorded. The ocular account of the case was furnished by Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7). Sajid Raza 399/HC (PW-1) stated that on 03.10.2022, the appellant was arrested by the Investigating Officer of the case and a personal search was conducted. Muhammad Nadeem 785/HC (PW-2) stated that on 30.09.2022, the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him sealed parcels, a motorcycle, burnt articles, sealed envelopes and on 01.10.2022, the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him sealed boxes and sealed envelopes and on 09.10.2022, he handed over the sealed

Sajid Raza 399/HC (PW-1) stated that on 03.10.2022, the appellant was arrested by the Investigating Officer of the case and a personal search was conducted. Muhammad Nadeem 785/HC (PW-2) stated that on 30.09.2022, the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him sealed parcels, a motorcycle, burnt articles, sealed envelopes and on 01.10.2022, the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him sealed boxes and sealed envelopes and on 09.10.2022, he handed over the sealed Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 5 boxes and sealed envelopes to Ejaz Hussain ASI (PW-10) for their onward transmission to Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore and on 12.10.2022, the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him a Universal Serial Bus (USB) storage device. Sajid Mahmood Hassan, ASI (PW-3) stated that on 30.09.2022, he got recorded the formal F.I.R. (Exh.PB). Faiz Alam (PW- 4) stated that on 30.09.2022 at about 12.07 p.m, the appellant had purchased petroleum from him. Abu Bakar Amir (PW-19) stated that on 30.09.2022 at about 12.00/12.15 p.m., the appellant had parked his motorcycle in front of his shop. Zainab Berkat 1562/LC (PW-14) stated that on 30.09.2022 she escorted the dead body of the deceased namely Gul Naz Bibi to the hospital and received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Medical Officer after the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased and on 01.10.2022, she got conducted the post mortem examinations of the dead bodies of Basra Bibi (deceased) and Shehnaz Bibi (deceased) and received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Woman Medical Officer. Muhammad Rafique Tracer (PW-18) prepared the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PBB). Abdul Kareem Inspector (PW-21) investigated the case from 30.09.2022 till 03.05.2023, arrested the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda on 03.10.2022 and detailed the facts of his investigation in his statement before the learned trial court. Muhammad Aslam, SI (PW-23) investigated the case on 30.09.2022 and detailed the facts of his investigation in his statement before the learned trial court. 6.

Abdul Kareem Inspector (PW-21) investigated the case from 30.09.2022 till 03.05.2023, arrested the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda on 03.10.2022 and detailed the facts of his investigation in his statement before the learned trial court. Muhammad Aslam, SI (PW-23) investigated the case on 30.09.2022 and detailed the facts of his investigation in his statement before the learned trial court. 6. The prosecution also got Dr. Sara Arshad (PW-11) examined, who on 30.09.2022 was posted as Woman Medical Officer at the Sheikh Zayed Hospital, Rahim Yar Khan and on the same day, conducted the post-mortem Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 6 examination of the dead body of Gul Naz wife of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased). Dr. Sara Arshad (PW-11), on examining the dead body of Gul Naz wife of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) observed as under:- “ A dead body of female aged approximately 50 years lying in supine position on mortuary table. The dead body was completely burnt from head to toe (100%). Due to severity of burn dead body was unidentifiable. Face was completely disfigured, body was stiff, swollen and in pugilistic attitude.............. I- DESCRIPTION OF INJURIES: (including findings on dissection) Following injuries were noted: Injury No.1 There was lacerated wound measuring 4 cm x 1.5 cm, bone was exposed, margin completely burnt, situated on the left side of head approaching upto frontal bone just 3.5 cm above the pinna of left ear. The underlying bone was healthy and no intracranial hemorrhage was seen in the skull. Note: This injury was not mentioned in police papers. Any bruise, laceration and mark of violence cannot be detected due to discoloration and burning of skin.................... OPINION After thorough internal and external examination, I am of the opinion that cause of death in this case was completely burnt (100% surface area) body was sufficient to cause death in normal course of life... However samples already mentioned had been sent to various labs for final report.

OPINION After thorough internal and external examination, I am of the opinion that cause of death in this case was completely burnt (100% surface area) body was sufficient to cause death in normal course of life... However samples already mentioned had been sent to various labs for final report. Final report was kept pending till the receiving of results from PFSA. Probable time that elapsed: (a) between injury and death: Within half an hour. (b) between death and post mortem: Within ten hours................ FINAL OPINION AFTER RECEIVING REPORTS FROM PFSA. After receiving and studying the above said reports, the cause of death is same which I have given earlier in my earlier opinion dated 30.9.2022, on the day of issuing postmortem examination report.” The prosecution also got Dr. Maria Ghaffar (PW-12) examined, who on 30.09.2022 was posted as Casualty Medical Officer at the Sheikh Zayed Hospital, Rahim Yar Khan and on the same day, examined Basra Bibi (then Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 7 injured and later deceased). Dr. Maria Ghaffar (PW-12), on examining Basra Bibi (then injured, later deceased) observed as under:- “On examination following injuries were noted: Injury No.1 Flame burn on all over body. On examination scalp hairs were burnt but not up to roots of hairs. Face was burn all over from front and back side. Facial tenderness seen. Front of chest and back of chest was burnt Involvement of skin was of mixed thickness Abdomen and lower back of body surface area was burnt all over. Front and back of each leg and foot were burnt. Genital area was also involved in flame burns Both arms from front and back up to tip of nail was burnt. According to rule of 9, head and neck (9%), Arms 9% each, genital area 1%, abdomen and lower back 9% each, total burn area was approximately 70% to 80%. Probable duration of Injuries 06 hours. Injuries were caused by flame burn.

Probable duration of Injuries 06 hours. Injuries were caused by flame burn. Possibility of fabrication was NIL. Injuries were kept under observation. On 30.09.2022, Dr. Maria Ghaffar (PW-12) also examined Shehnaz Bibi (then injured, later deceased). Dr. Maria Ghaffar (PW-12), on examining Shehnaz Bibi (then injured, later deceased) observed as under:- “Injury No.1 Flame burn on all over body. On examination scalp hairs intact Face was burnt all over and edema present. Front of chest and back of chest was burnt and skin was of mixed thickness. Abdomen and lower back of body surface area was burnt all over. Front and back of each leg and foot were burnt. Both arms from front and back up to tip of nail was burnt.” Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 8 The prosecution also got Dr. Amara Irum (PW-13) examined, who on 01.10.2022 was posted as Woman Medical Officer at the Sheikh Zayed Hospital, Rahim Yar Khan and on the same day, conducted the post-mortem examination of the dead body of Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased). Dr. Amara Irum (PW-13), on examining the dead body of Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased) observed as under:- “I- DESCRIPTION OF INJURIES: (including findings on dissection) Following injuries were noted: The dead body was burnt from head to toe approximately 90% of surface area of the body, skin was discolored and peel off from multiple sides due to severe burn. so no injury bruise and mark of violence cannot be detected............... OPINION After conducting thorough autopsy, I am of the opinion that cause of death in this case was completely burnt (90% surface area) body was sufficient to cause death in normal course of life.” On 01.10.2022, Dr.

Amara Irum (PW-13), on examining the dead body of Basra Bibi wife of Akbar (deceased) observed as under:- “I- DESCRIPTION OF INJURIES: (including findings on dissection) Following injuries were noted: The dead body was burnt from head to toe approximately 90% of surface area of the body, skin was discolored and peel off from multiple sides due to severe burn. so no injury bruise and mark of violence cannot be detected............... OPINION After conducting thorough autopsy, I am of the opinion that cause of death in this case was completely burnt (90% surface area) body was sufficient to cause death in normal course of life.” On 01.10.2022, Dr. Amara Irum (PW-13) also conducted the post-mortem examination of the dead body of Shehnaz Bibi daughter of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased). Dr. Amara Irum (PW-13), on examining the dead body of Shehnaz Bibi daughter of Khursheed Ahmad (deceased) observed as under:- “1- DESCRIPTION OF INJURIES: (including findings on dissection) Following injuries were noted: The dead body was burnt from head to toe approximately 85% of surface area of the body, skin was discolored and peel off from multiple sides due to severe burn, so no injury bruise and mark of violence cannot be detected................ OPINION Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 9 After conducting thorough autopsy, I am of the opinion that cause of death in this case was completely burnt (85% surface area) body was sufficient to cause death in normal course of life.” The prosecution also got Dr. Muhammad Uzair Israr (PW-9) examined who on 03.10.2022 was posted as Medical Officer at Sheikh Zayed Hospital, Rahim Yar Khan and on the same day had examined the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda and observed the presence of burn injury on the body of the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda and issued the Medico Legal Examination Certificate (Exh.PH/1). 7. On 20.06.2023, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General gave up the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Ferman, Ghulam Farid, Muhammad Irshad, Waqas Iqbal, Sadiq Mahmood constable and Makhna Gee constable as being unnecessary and closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the reports of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PHH, Exh.PJJ, Exh.PJJ/1 and Exh.PJJ/2) and certified copies of documents (Exh.PKK/1-4). 8.

Muhammad Uzair Israr (PW-9) examined who on 03.10.2022 was posted as Medical Officer at Sheikh Zayed Hospital, Rahim Yar Khan and on the same day had examined the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda and observed the presence of burn injury on the body of the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda and issued the Medico Legal Examination Certificate (Exh.PH/1). 7. On 20.06.2023, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General gave up the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Ferman, Ghulam Farid, Muhammad Irshad, Waqas Iqbal, Sadiq Mahmood constable and Makhna Gee constable as being unnecessary and closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the reports of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PHH, Exh.PJJ, Exh.PJJ/1 and Exh.PJJ/2) and certified copies of documents (Exh.PKK/1-4). 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, the appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda replied that he was innocent and had been falsely involved in the case due to suspicion. The appellant namely Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda further stated that he had not committed any offence and was not involved in the occurrence in any manner. The appellant namely Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 10 Muhammad Sultan son of Jind Wadda opted not to get himself examined under section 340 Cr.P.C. and did not adduce any evidence in his defence. 9. On the conclusion of the trial, the learned Judge, Anti-Terrorism Court, Bahawalpur Division, Bahawalpur, convicted and sentenced the appellant as referred to above. 10. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellant precisely was that the whole case was fabricated and false. The learned counsel for the appellant submitted that the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible and relevant evidence. The learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the statements of prosecution witnesses were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellant also submitted that the appellant had no connection with the occurrence. The learned counsel for the appellant also argued that the recoveries were full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value and result of fake proceedings.

The learned counsel for the appellant also submitted that the appellant had no connection with the occurrence. The learned counsel for the appellant also argued that the recoveries were full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value and result of fake proceedings. The learned counsel for the appellant finally submitted that the prosecution had totally failed to prove the case against the appellant beyond the shadow of a doubt. 11. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant of the case contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond the shadow of any uncertainty by producing independent witnesses. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant of the case further argued that the appellant namely Muhammad Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 11 Sultan son of Jind Wadda was responsible for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of all the three deceased. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant of the case contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses to substitute the real offender with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant of the case prayed for the rejection of the appeal. 12. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellant, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General, the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant of the case and with their assistance, perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial. 13. In the instant unfortunate incident, three unfortunate women, namely Gul Naz Bibi, Shehnaz Bibi and Basra Bibi lost their lives after having been burnt to death. The deceased namely Gul Naz Bibi, Shehnaz Bibi and Basra Bibi suffered from massive burns and died painful and miserable deaths.

In the instant unfortunate incident, three unfortunate women, namely Gul Naz Bibi, Shehnaz Bibi and Basra Bibi lost their lives after having been burnt to death. The deceased namely Gul Naz Bibi, Shehnaz Bibi and Basra Bibi suffered from massive burns and died painful and miserable deaths. The prosecution in order to prove the ocular account, got Khursheed Ahmad (PW- 5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7) examined. The prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) and Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) were related to the deceased. The deceased namely Gul Naz Bibi was the wife of the prosecution witness namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) and the deceased namely Shehnaz Bibi was the daughter of the prosecution witness namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) whereas the deceased Basra Bibi was the sister of Gul Naz Bibi (deceased). The prosecution witness namely Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) is the maternal nephew of Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5). It Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 12 is also a fact that the other prosecution witness namely Muhammad Asif (PW-7) was not even related to either the deceased or to the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) and Amjad Iqbal (PW-6). More important is the fact that the appellant was the estranged husband of Shehnaz Bibi (deceased) and in this manner was very well known to all the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7). It is also a fact that the place of occurrence was the shop of Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), constructed within the boundary of the house of Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5). All the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW- 7) explained their presence at the place of occurrence categorically. We have noted that all the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7), in a very natural and forthright manner, narrated the occurrence and gave each and every feature of the same with consistency.

All the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW- 7) explained their presence at the place of occurrence categorically. We have noted that all the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7), in a very natural and forthright manner, narrated the occurrence and gave each and every feature of the same with consistency. The presence of the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW- 7) at the place of occurrence was quite probable and natural. As mentioned above, the occurrence in the present case took place inside the shop of Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) constructed within the boundary of the house of Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5). All the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7) explained that on the day of occurrence, they were attracted to the place of the occurrence on the hue and cry of the deceased women and were present at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence. All the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7) were cross-examined at length with regard to their arrival at the Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 13 place of occurrence and all the said prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7) gave consistent and unswerving evidence in this regard.

All the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7) explained that on the day of occurrence, they were attracted to the place of the occurrence on the hue and cry of the deceased women and were present at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence. All the prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7) were cross-examined at length with regard to their arrival at the Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 13 place of occurrence and all the said prosecution witnesses namely Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5), Amjad Iqbal (PW-6) and Muhammad Asif (PW-7) gave consistent and unswerving evidence in this regard. During cross-examination, Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) was questioned with regard to him being attracted to the place of occurrence and Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) explained that when he saw the appellant carrying a bottle of petroleum with him going towards his house, he immediately not only followed him but also raised hue and cry and stated as under:- “ As I saw accused Sultan passing me at Pull Gharib Shah holding bottle of petrol with him, I made hue and cry. ” Similarly, Muhammad Asif (PW-7) also explained during cross-examination that as he was going towards the house of Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) for purchase of certain grocery items, he not only saw the appellant carrying a bottle of petroleum in his hand present inside the shop of Khursheed Ahmad (PW-5) but was also attracted to the said shop after hearing hue and cry and stated as under:- “I was already in the way towards the shop of complainant for purchasing grocery item from Northern side, when I heard hue and cry. ” It is also a fact that after the incident Abdul Kareem, Inspector (PW-21), the Investigating Officer of the case, collected from the place of the incident the brown colored, wet, half burnt, 'Dupatta' (P-7), one pair of shoe (P8/1-2) belonging to Gul Naaz (deceased) and also took into possession two burnt packets of milk 'Tarang (P9/1-2), two burnt packets of cigarettes (P10/1-2), one burnt box of candies (P11), one burnt packet of Lays chips (P12), burnt biscuits Zeera (P13), burnt pampers of zero size (P14), one burnt packet of Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 14 Vital tea (P15), one burnt packet of Nimko (P16), burnt packet of Mehndi (P17) and burnt thread roles (P18),one purple color burnt iron Pipe with length of 2 feet (P25), the burnt cot Undeniably, it is an admitted fact that provisions of Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 are co-related and ancillary to the substantive offence, which in all fairness is the provision of section 302, P.P.C. The gravity of said substantive offence can lead the court to the conclusion Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 24 whether the provisions of Anti-terrorism Act, 1997 are attracted or not. The Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 is a special law enacted with a special intent and purpose, which can be gathered from the bare reading of Preamble of the said Act. It is a principle of legislation that the preamble of any enactment is always considered as the grundnorm of the legislation, which expresses the postulates to attract the said provision of the enactment according to its scope of legislation. As stated above, the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997, is a special enactment and a special enactment needs to be taken in perspective of its own object. Any departure from the same would be a negation of its object and spirit. A preamble of a statute explains the very purpose and underlying philosophy behind the enactment. To better understand the scheme of Anti- terrorism Act, 1997, it would be in order to reproduce the Preamble of the Act, which reads as under:- "An act to provide for the prevention of terrorism, sectarian violence and for speedy trial of heinous offences; Whereas it is expedient to provide for the prevention of terrorism, sectarian violence and for speedy trial of heinous offences and for matters connected therewith and incidental thereto;” A bare perusal of the Preamble shows that the basic purpose behind the enactment of the Anti-terrorism Act, 1997, was to prevent, (i) terrorism, (ii) sectarian violence, and (iii) for speedy trial of heinous offences.

The resume of our legislative developments in the field of terrorism shows, as already observed in the case of Basharat Ali (supra), that with different laws and definitions of terrorist act or terrorism the emphasis has been shifting from one criterion to another including the gravity of the act, lethal nature of the weapon used, plurality of culprits, number of victims, impact created by the act and effect of fear and insecurity brought about or likely to be created in the society by the action. The last definition of a 'terrorist act' contained in section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 squarely focused on the effect of fear and insecurity intended to be created by the act or actually created by the act or the act having the potential of creating such an effect of fear and insecurity in the society. It, however, appears that subsequently the legislature did not feel convinced of the aptness or correctness of that definition and resultantly the erstwhile definition of a 'terrorist act' contained in section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 was repealed and a totally fresh and new definition of 'terrorism' was introduced through an amended section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997.

The last definition of a 'terrorist act' contained in section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 squarely focused on the effect of fear and insecurity intended to be created by the act or actually created by the act or the act having the potential of creating such an effect of fear and insecurity in the society. It, however, appears that subsequently the legislature did not feel convinced of the aptness or correctness of that definition and resultantly the erstwhile definition of a 'terrorist act' contained in section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 was repealed and a totally fresh and new definition of 'terrorism' was introduced through an amended section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997. The legislature had probably realized by then that an effect of an act may not always be a correct indicator of the nature of such an act as every crime, especially of violence against person or property, does create some sense of fear and insecurity in some section of the society and a definition of terrorism Capital Sentence Reference No.02 of 2023 Criminal Appeal No. 358-J of 2023 30 based upon the magnitude or potential of an effect created or intended to be created or having a potential of creating would necessarily require a premature, speculative and imaginary quantification of the effect so as to determine the nature of the act in order to decide about the jurisdiction of a criminal court to try such an act. That surely was an unsure test and the result of such a premature, speculative and presumptive test could vary from court to court and from Judge to Judge reminding a legal scholar of the Star Chamber and the early days of a Court of Equity in England where equity was said to vary with the size of the Chancellor's foot. The new definition of 'terrorism' introduced through the amended section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 as it stands today appears to be closer to the universally understood concept of terrorism besides being easier to understand and apply. The earlier emphasis on the speculative effect of the act has now given way to a clearly defined mens rea and actus reus. The amended clause (b) of subsection of section 6 now specifies the 'design' and clause (c) of subsection of section 6 earmarks the 'purpose' which should be the motivation for the act and the actus reus has been clearly mentioned in subsection of section 6 and now it is only when the actus reus specified in subsection of section 6 is accompanied by the requisite mens rea provided for in clause (b) or clause (c) of subsection of section 6 that an action can be termed as 'terrorism'.

The amended clause (b) of subsection of section 6 now specifies the 'design' and clause (c) of subsection of section 6 earmarks the 'purpose' which should be the motivation for the act and the actus reus has been clearly mentioned in subsection of section 6 and now it is only when the actus reus specified in subsection of section 6 is accompanied by the requisite mens rea provided for in clause (b) or clause (c) of subsection of section 6 that an action can be termed as 'terrorism'. Thus, it is no longer the fear or insecurity actually created or intended to be created or likely to be created which would determine whether the action qualifies to be termed as terrorism or not but it is now the intent and motivation behind the action which would be determinative of the issue irrespective of the fact whether any fear and insecurity was actually created or not. After this amendment in section 6 an action can now be termed as terrorism if the use or threat of that action is designed to coerce and intimidate or overawe the Government or the public or a section of the public or community or sect, etc. or if such action is designed to create a sense of fear or insecurity in the society or the use or threat is made for the purpose of advancing a religious, sectarian or ethnic cause, etc. Now creating fear or insecurity in the society is not by itself terrorism unless the motive itself is to create fear or insecurity in the society and not when fear or insecurity is just a byproduct, a fallout or an unintended consequence of a private crime. In the last definition the focus was on the action and its result whereas in the present definition the emphasis appears to be on the motivation and objective and not on the result. Through this amendment the legislature seems to have finally appreciated that mere shock, horror, dread or disgust created or likely to be created in the society does not transform a private crime into terrorism but terrorism as an 'ism' is a totally different concept which denotes commission of a crime with the design or purpose of destabilizing the government, disturbing the society or hurting a section of the society with a view to achieve objectives which are essentially political, ideological or religious.

In the last definition the focus was on the action and its result whereas in the present definition the emphasis appears to be on the motivation and objective and not on the result. This approach also appears to be in harmony with the emerging international perspective and perception about terrorism. The international perception is also becoming clearer on the point that a violent activity against civilians that has no political, ideological or religious aims is just an act of criminal delinquency, a felony, or simply an act of insanity unrelated to terrorism. This metamorphosis in the anti-terrorism law in our country has brought about a sea change in the whole concept as we have understood it in the past and it is, therefore, of paramount importance for all concerned to understand this conceptual modification and transformation in its true perspective. 16.
